findings.title: Sidecar uses index.md as single memory entry point
findings.detail: On Activation directs loading `_bmad/memory/gamma-specialist-sidecar/index.md` as the entry point before deeper sidecar files, matching selective memory loading. Fallback to `references/init.md` avoids loading a full sidecar when none exists.

findings.title: Capability table loads references only when a route is taken
findings.detail: Internal capabilities (PR, SG, QA, ES, CT, SM) each map to a single reference file loaded on demand rather than loading all references at activation. Reduces baseline token use and keeps context aligned to the active task.

findings.title: On Activation lists independent reads without parallel batching guidance
findings.detail: The block loads `_bmad/config.yaml`, optional `config.user.yaml`, `references/memory-system.md`, sidecar `index.md` or `init.md`, and `style_guide.yaml` as a linear list. These I/Os have no hard ordering among several items; serial tool rounds add latency versus one batched parallel read pass. Estimated savings: one fewer assistant turn on cold start when tools support parallelism.
findings.action: Add an explicit line: when using file tools, issue parallel reads for config files, memory-system.md, sidecar index (or init), and style_guide.yaml in one round unless a parse result gates the next step.

findings.title: Doc refresh can batch parallel Ref MCP fetches for multiple parameter pages
findings.detail: After API changes are detected, scanning affected parameter pages via Ref MCP is described as a sequential workflow. Multiple independent URLs are typical; sequential ref_read_url calls multiply wall-clock time. Batching independent URL reads in one message reduces latency in proportion to page count.
findings.action: When multiple parameter docs need refresh, fetch all affected URLs in parallel in a single tool batch, then merge updates into `parameter-catalog.md`.

findings.title: First-interaction onboarding steps are fully sequential
findings.detail: First interactive activation lists Gamma connectivity check, then style guide read, then report. The style guide read does not depend on API success textually; partial parallelization may save a small fraction of first-run time.
findings.action: Where the runtime allows, run connectivity preflight and `style_guide.yaml` read in parallel, then synthesize the greeting from both outcomes.

findings.title: Style guide YAML read overlaps with style-guide-integration.md
findings.detail: `parameter-recommendation.md` and `references/style-guide-integration.md` both instruct reading `state/config/style_guide.yaml` at invocation. Tasks that route through PR then SG (or vice versa) may repeat the same read in one turn, wasting tokens and I/O.
findings.action: State in SKILL or cross-references: one read of `tool_parameters.gamma` per task satisfies both capabilities; subsequent steps reuse the loaded block.

prepass_integration.notes: No script-flagged sequential chains, loop patterns, or subagent-chain violations; LLM review focused on prose workflow and memory/resource strategy.

summary.assessment: Pre-pass was clean; the skill is strong on selective memory and capability-scoped references. Main improvements are explicit cold-start read batching, parallel doc-refresh fetches when many URLs change, and deduplicating style-guide reads across PR/SG.
